{"problem_standardized_math":"For the function\n\\[\nf(x)=\\frac{x_1x_2\\sin x_3+e^{x_1x_2}}{x_3},\n\\]\nwith intermediate variables defined by\n\\[\n\\begin{aligned}\nx_4 &= x_1x_2,\\\\\nx_5 &= \\sin x_3,\\\\\nx_6 &= e^{x_4},\\\\\nx_7 &= x_4x_5,\\\\\nx_8 &= x_6+x_7,\\\\\nx_9 &= \\frac{x_8}{x_3},\n\\end{aligned}\n\\]\ncompute the partial derivatives \\(\\partial x_j/\\partial x_i\\) from these expressions and use them to verify the numerical values assigned to the arcs in Figure 8.3 at the evaluation point \\(x=(1,2,\\pi/2)^T\\). Then work through the remaining details of the reverse sweep process, indicating the order in which the nodes become finalized."}
